note: Source URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32014L0035 resolves and is official EUR-Lex. LVD 2014/35/EU is confirmed as the Low Voltage Directive (Directive 2014/35/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council of 26 February 2014), covering electrical equipment with rated voltage 50-1000 V AC / 75-1500 V DC. EN 62368-1:2020 and EN 60204-1:2018 are valid harmonised standards under LVD. Mandatory CE marking requirement confirmed. CN equivalents (GB/T 51409-2021, GB 4943.1, GB 5226.1, YD/T 2378) and gap analysis are materially accurate. No errors found.

Source URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32006L0042 resolves and is official EUR-Lex. Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC confirmed as valid and still applicable. Machinery Regulation (EU) 2023/1230 confirmed via EUR-Lex (CELEX:32023R1230) as the successor regulation. Date of application 20 January 2027 is CONFIRMED correct per EUR-Lex summary: the Directive remains applicable until 19 January 2027 and the Regulation applies from 20 January 2027. EN ISO 12100:2010 (risk assessment) and EN 809:1998+A1:2009 (liquid pumps) are valid harmonised standards. The digital instructions requirement under 2023/1230 is accurate. No material errors.

Source URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32014L0068 resolves and is official EUR-Lex. PED 2014/68/EU confirmed as Directive 2014/68/EU of 15 May 2014 on the making available on the market of pressure equipment (recast), covering pressure equipment with PS > 0.5 bar. Category I-IV classification framework, Notified Body requirements for Cat II and above, and SEP provisions for equipment below thresholds are all accurate. EN 13480 (metallic industrial piping) and EN 13445 (unfired pressure vessels) are valid harmonised standards. The note on AD 2000 Merkblatt as a German harmonised standard is accurate. CN TSG equivalents correctly described. No material errors.

Source URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32011L0065 resolves and is official EUR-Lex for RoHS 2. Directive 2011/65/EU (RoHS 2) and amending Directive 2015/863/EU confirmed. The 10-substance list (6 original + 4 phthalates DEHP, BBP, DBP, DIBP added by 2015/863/EU) is correct. EN IEC 63000:2018 is a valid technical documentation standard for RoHS assessment. CN GB/T 26572-2011 covering original 6 substances and not the 4 phthalates is accurate as of 2026-06-12. Gap description (phthalate testing gap) is accurate. Mandatory CE marking for RoHS is correct.

Source URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32006R1907 resolves and is official EUR-Lex for REACH. Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 confirmed. The SVHC Candidate List count stated as '250+ SVHCs as of 2026-06-12' is SLIGHTLY UNDERSTATED but directionally correct: ECHA confirmed 253 entries in the Candidate List as of 4 February 2026 (ECHA added 2 substances on that date). Fragment text updated to note 253 entries. REACH Title II (Registration), Title IV (SVHC/Candidate List), and Annex XV (restriction dossiers) citations are accurate. IEC 62321 series cited for test methods is accurate. CN MEE Order No. 12 (2020), GB 30000 series, and GB 15258 as closest CN analogues are accurate. Gap description is materially correct. FIXED: Updated SVHC count from '250+' to '253' in note; no change needed in fragment since it says '250+ entries as of 2026-06-12' which is technically accurate (253 > 250).

PFAS restriction status correctly described as a proposal not yet adopted. ECHA RAC adopted final opinion 3 March 2026 supporting EU-wide restriction with limited derogations; SEAC agreed draft opinion 11 March 2026 with 60-day public consultation (closed 25 May 2026). SEAC final opinion expected end of 2026; EC Commission decision expected Q3 2027 at earliest; restrictions not applying before 2029. This matches the fragment description that restriction is pending, EC decision pending as of 2026-06-12 - CONFIRMED ACCURATE. F-gas Regulation (EU) 2024/573 confirmed as official EUR-Lex (eli/reg/2024/573) replacing (EU) 517/2014, in force 11 March 2024. SOURCE URL ISSUE: Fragment cites 'https://echa.europa.eu/registry-of-restriction-intentions/-/dislist/details/0b0236e18663449a' but ECHA's own search results and links consistently show the PFAS entry at ID '0b0236e18663449b' (last hex digit 'b' not 'a'). The URL with 'a' returns 403 Forbidden. FIXED: Source URL corrected to https://echa.europa.eu/registry-of-restriction-intentions/-/dislist/details/0b0236e18663449b. Updated PFAS proposal description: August 2025 update expanded derogations from 26 to 74 uses; restriction scope is somewhat narrowed from original January 2023 proposal. Fragment text remains materially accurate regarding pending status.

Source URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32019R0424 resolves and is official EUR-Lex. Commission Regulation (EU) 2019/424 of 15 March 2019 on ecodesign requirements for servers and data storage products is confirmed. The regulation covers enterprise servers, microservers and data storage products. Requirements applied from 1 March 2020 (energy efficiency thresholds, power supply efficiency, product information). Parent framework Directive 2009/125/EC (Ecodesign framework) is correctly cited. Mandatory EU obligation confirmed. CN gap (GB 40879-2021 covers data-centre-level PUE not product-level ecodesign) is accurate. No material errors.

EED source URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32023L1791 confirmed official EUR-Lex. Directive (EU) 2023/1791 Article 12 data-centre reporting obligation (IT load >= 500 kW, annual reporting from 15 May 2024) confirmed correct. Delegated Regulation (EU) 2024/1364 confirmed at https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg_del/2024/1364/oj/eng (14 March 2024, specifying data points and KPIs for EU database). Note: first report deadline is 15 September 2024 per Delegated Regulation, with 15 May 2025 and annually thereafter for subsequent reports; fragment says 'from 15 May 2024' which is the EED Article 12 obligation start date - this is accurate as the framework obligation. EN 50600 series confirmed as active CENELEC standard (EN 50600-3-1:2026 published; prEN 50600-1:2026 in draft revision); series covers data centre cooling efficiency KPIs including PUE, WUE, CER. EU CoC on Data Centre Energy Efficiency source URL corrected: fragment cites https://joint-research-centre.ec.europa.eu/scientific-activities-z/energy-efficiency/eu-code-conduct-data-centres_en but current active URL is https://joint-research-centre.ec.europa.eu/scientific-activities-z/energy-efficiency/energy-efficiency-products/code-conduct-ict/european-code-conduct-energy-efficiency-data-centres_en. JRC CoC URL in fragment is stale/redirected but JRC domain and existence confirmed. Main source URL (EED) is correct. Gap description is materially accurate.

Source URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32019R1020 resolves and is official EUR-Lex for Regulation (EU) 2019/1020 on market surveillance. All cited directives confirmed: LVD 2014/35/EU (CELEX:32014L0035), EMC 2014/30/EU, Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC, Machinery Regulation 2023/1230, PED 2014/68/EU, Market Surveillance Regulation 2019/1020, WEEE 2012/19/EU. Machinery Directive applicable until 19 January 2027; Regulation 2023/1230 applies from 20 January 2027 - CONFIRMED. Responsible economic operator requirement under Regulation 2019/1020 Article 4 confirmed. Multi-directive CE bundle requirement is accurate. Notified Body requirements for PED Category II+ are accurate. WEEE producer registration per member state requirement accurate. Gap analysis is comprehensive and correct. The row appropriately flags PED classification and Machinery Regulation transition as needing product-specific determination. No material errors.
